key_insight: A self-supervised mask-aware Transformer can learn robust force estimation from multi-channel FBG sensors even when some channels are missing or fractured.
engineering_relevance: This enables more fault-tolerant, calibration-light sensing for minimally invasive surgical robotics, where catheter-scale sensors must survive complex deformation and intermittent damage.
method: Pretrain a Transformer with masked-channel reconstruction on unlabeled sensor streams.
Fine-tune for force regression using both clean and corrupted views plus a dynamic corruption curriculum.
Add a heteroscedastic uncertainty head to predict per-axis confidence in one forward pass.
evidence_signal: On an 8-channel catheter-scale FBG dataset, the model achieved 0.0066 N RMSE nominally and 0.0126 N with severe 4-channel failures, outperforming a 255-model bank at 0.0154 N under 4-channel loss.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative corruption patterns during self-supervised pretraining and curriculum design, so unseen failure modes may still require additional validation.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Mask-Aware Transformers for Fault-Tolerant FBG Force Sensing in Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics
